description: New and corrected references for Iteration 4. References already in iter_003/context/literature.md are not repeated here unless corrections are needed.

new_references.title: The Marginal Value of Adaptive Gradient Methods in Machine Learning
new_references.key_contribution: Empirical and theoretical evidence that adaptive gradient methods (Adam, AdaGrad, RMSProp) generalize worse than SGD with momentum on diverse tasks. Constructs pathological case where Adam achieves ~50% test error while SGD achieves 0%.
new_references.relevance: Foundational reference for why dynamic WD may behave differently under SGD vs AdamW. The Phi Invariance Conjecture (null result under AdamW) aligns with this paper's finding that adaptive methods reduce sensitivity to regularization perturbations. Needed in Discussion section.

new_references.title: L2 Regularization versus Batch and Weight Normalization
new_references.key_contribution: Shows L2 regularization under batch/weight normalization acts as effective learning rate (ELR) modulator, not explicit regularizer. First derivation of ELR hypothesis. Adam only partially eliminates normalization-induced LR sensitivity.
new_references.relevance: Foundational reference for WD-as-ELR-modifier interpretation. Connects to D'Angelo et al. 2024 and provides historical grounding for the Phi Invariance mechanistic argument. Needed in Related Work section.

new_references.title: Equivalence Tests: A Practical Primer for t Tests, Correlations, and Meta-Analyses
new_references.key_contribution: Establishes TOST (Two One-Sided Tests) as standard methodology for providing evidence of practical equivalence. Sets upper and lower equivalence bounds; when both one-sided nulls are rejected, effect is affirmatively within equivalence bounds. Fundamentally different from standard NHST (p>0.05 ≠ equivalence).
new_references.relevance: Required for addressing Iteration 3 reviewer M3 (statistical power and equivalence testing). Phi Invariance Conjecture needs TOST support, not just failed-to-reject null. Needed in Methods/Statistical Analysis section.
new_references.implementation: Python: pingouin.tost(); R: TOSTER package. Bounds for CIFAR accuracy comparison: ±0.5% (absolute), equivalent to Cohen's d ≈ 0.3 with typical within-seed variation of ~0.15%.

new_references.title: Adaptive Adam-based Optimizers Using Second-Order Weight Decoupling and Gradient-Aware Weight Decay for Vision Transformer
new_references.key_contribution: Proposes adaptive Adam optimizers with second-order weight decoupling and gradient-aware WD specifically for ViT. Validates on ImageNet classification. Shows gradient-aware WD provides measurable gains for ViTs.
new_references.relevance: 2025 paper in gradient-aware WD space for ViT. Partially overlaps with Alignment-Aware WD axis of our framework. Must cite in related work. Does NOT provide unified framework or BEM/CSI/AIS metrics.
new_references.novelty_risk: LOW — narrow scope, no unified framework, no null-result analysis

new_references.title: Straight to Zero: Why Linearly Decaying the Learning Rate to Zero Works Best for LLMs
new_references.key_contribution: Linear decay-to-zero (D2Z) consistently outperforms cosine decay for compute-optimal LLM training. Derived from AdamW-as-EMA interpretation. 60% compute savings demonstrated at 610M parameter scale.
new_references.relevance: Shows WD scheduling interacts strongly with LR schedule shape. WD appears as EMA window length reciprocal under D2Z interpretation. Suggests dynamic WD = dynamic EMA window — natural scheduling motivation. Informs Iter 4 experimental baselines.

new_references.title: Universal Dynamics of Warmup Stable Decay
new_references.key_contribution: WSD (Warmup-Stable-Decay) training dynamics are universal across transformers and CNNs. Key finding: switching off WD during high-variance cooldown phases (square, mirror cosine) significantly improves performance.
new_references.relevance: Shows WD-schedule interaction is real and non-trivial at the cooldown phase. Implies even 'constant WD' baselines may be suboptimal if WD should be turned off during cooldown. Informs Iter 4 baseline design.
new_references.status: NEW — add to Experimental Design and Discussion

new_references.title: On the Origin of Implicit Regularization in Stochastic Gradient Descent
new_references.key_contribution: SGD with random shuffling follows gradient flow on a modified loss = original loss + implicit regularizer proportional to norm of minibatch gradients, scaled by learning_rate/batch_size. Formally derives implicit regularization mechanism.
new_references.relevance: Provides theoretical foundation for discussing why SGD has different sensitivity to WD perturbations than AdamW — SGD's implicit regularizer is gradient-norm-based, which interacts with explicit WD differently than AdamW's ℓ∞ implicit constraint.

new_references.title: How Neural Networks Learn the Support is an Implicit Regularization Effect of SGD
new_references.key_contribution: Weights interacting with irrelevant data components converge to zero if and only if weight decay is added to SGD training. Shows WD is *necessary* for certain implicit regularization effects — contradicts pure 'dynamics modifier' framing.
new_references.relevance: Provides nuanced view: WD can still be an explicit regularizer in certain settings (SGD + feature selection). Strengthens the argument that the Phi Invariance Conjecture is optimizer-specific (holds for AdamW, may not hold for SGD).

corrections_to_iter3_references.original_citation: Kosson et al. arXiv 2305.17212 / NeurIPS 2023 Workshop
corrections_to_iter3_references.correction: Published in ICML 2024 Proceedings, volume 235, pages 25333-25369. The NeurIPS 2023 Workshop was the preliminary version. Final venue should be cited as ICML 2024.

confirmed_references_unchanged.note: WD > muP for LR transfer, verified correct
